body_txt: A character who has such pervasive, intrusive, or extremely noticeable personality quirks that only an idiot would hire them, but who remains employed because they're so competent only an idiot would fire them. A form of Ultimate Job Security . Coworkers, superiors, and friends are willing to tolerate or overlook these informalities (e.g. a trial attorney who insists on wearing pink bunny ears in the courtroom) as long as the job gets done. Is often a Hyper-Competent Sidekick , where their absence will result in at least a small implosion of productivity. This is a cost/benefit deal: as long as John Coney, Esq. keeps winning cases, his favorite pair of ears will be brushed and ready at all times. In any case, their eccentricities may be the result of Intrusive Thoughts and/or Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny! . These quirks are highly prone to get them Diagnosed by the Audience . Much like the Ditzy Genius , such characters run on a strange mixture of brilliant and bizarre. Their quirks don't seem to be intrinsic to success, a la Success Through Insanity , nor do their odd ways interfere much with their daily functioning . Really, only the Naïve Newcomer and total strangers will question them, as everyone who actually works with them have long learned to adjust accordingly . It's also not enough to be a Consummate Professional at work but have some weird fetishes at home, there needs to be a certain amount of uncomfortable overlap. Compare the Insufferable Genius , a Jerkass only tolerated because they are the smartest person in the room. The Insufferable Genius might not respond unless addressed as "sir" or " Dr. Smith "; the Bunny-Ears Lawyer might not answer to anything but " Grand Duchess Abigail Chester Wilson Snapdragon Lemmywinks Brian Brain McFisticuffs the Negative 10 to the Fifth Power ". Imagine how indispensable someone would have to be for their coworkers to put up with that all day, but forced to stick around. There are lots of Bunny-Ears Lawyers out there (we had to make subpages because of how many), mainly because having the character be an expert allows the writers to plausibly put them in any situation they want while their quirks give them originality and charm. It is also a form of Wish Fulfillment , especially in professional settings or societies that place a high value on conformity and "going by the book." There is Truth in Television to all of this. No two employees have the exact same skillset, the more one employee can do makes them more valuable to the company. If a particular skillset is conversely rarer and more difficult to find, leadership will sometimes ignore or placate quirks so long as they deliver high quality work, especially if it doesn't compromise the job at hand. Although you're unlikely to see something as extreme as an actual bunny ears-wearing lawyer, you will find plenty of examples of people who remain employed despite their personal peculiarities. Indeed, they may be on the receiving end of Complimenting the Oddball . If this character is part of a fuzzy-eared flock instead of being an eccentric stand-out, you've likely got a Ragtag Bunch of Misfits . For situations where the strange guy is the one in charge (and uses their strange behavior to run their business) see The Wonka . Uniformed examples may overlap with the Military Maverick . A common way of subverting Obviously Unqualified . If they're a machine of some kind, see Eccentric A.I. . Not to be confused with the Ambulance Chaser , though they may overlap in some cases. Compare the Indispensable Scoundrel , who is extremely competent but is shady and disliked as opposed to quirky. Contrast Consummate Professional , who will care about his behavior and appearance as much as his work discipline and skill. Luckily, they're rarely Ax-Crazy . (Not never.) For an ultra manly-man who pulls off some girly hobby and nobody minds, take a refresher in the fact that Real Men Wear Pink . Remember: In order for a character to qualify he must keep his job through sheer unquestionable competence. Sometimes you have incompetent characters that happen to be quirky and keep their jobs through means that have nothing to do with their competence. That is not this trope. If they are actually competent yet are seen as "renegade" due to their unusual behavior or philosophy and often are at risk of being fired (if they could find a legitimate way to get rid of you), you are looking at The Last DJ . note The characteristics of a Last DJ that rub his colleagues the wrong way are usually more related to elements related to work ethic, such as integrity, authenticity, and defying the bureaucracy, politics, or social cliques of the workplace in question, not mere self-indulgent quirks and eccentricities that half the workplace enjoys anyway. The character should ideally have some external observation about their quirk or eccentricity as a potential issue in the workplace, not just be slightly more informal than their colleagues and never commented on. The quirks should ideally be a deliberate indulgence confidently relished by the person in question knowing they won't suffer consequence, not merely due to Social Awkwardness , some unspecified mental disorder, or Single-Issue Psychology . Not to be confused with Kangaroo Court . See also the Exotic Detective and Saved by the Awesome . A Quirky Doctor might qualify if they are also competent. Can be a part of a Crazy Workplace if most others are just as wacky as them. Might engage in Recent Event Nostalgia . Also prone to Unfinished Chore Trouble . Compare Irresponsible Mentor , for a capable teacher with a problematic personal life. noreallife
